   597→printf "${SLATE_600}────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────${RESET}\n"
   598→
   599→# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
   600→# LINE 1: CONTEXT
   601→# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
   602→
   603→# Format duration
   604→duration_sec=$((duration_ms / 1000))
   605→if   [ "$duration_sec" -ge 3600 ]; then time_display="$((duration_sec / 3600))h$((duration_sec % 3600 / 60))m"
   606→elif [ "$duration_sec" -ge 60 ];   then time_display="$((duration_sec / 60))m$((duration_sec % 60))s"
   607→else time_display="${duration_sec}s"
   608→fi
   609→
   610→# Context display - scale to compaction threshold if configured
   611→context_max="${context_max:-200000}"
   612→max_k=$((context_max / 1000))
   613→
   614→# Read compaction threshold from settings (default 100 = no scaling)
   615→COMPACTION_THRESHOLD=$(jq -r '.contextDisplay.compactionThreshold // 100' "$SETTINGS_FILE" 2>/dev/null)
   616→COMPACTION_THRESHOLD="${COMPACTION_THRESHOLD:-100}"
   617→
   618→# Get raw percentage from Claude Code
   619→raw_pct="${context_pct%%.*}"  # Remove decimals
   620→[ -z "$raw_pct" ] && raw_pct=0
   621→
   622→# Scale percentage: if threshold is 62, then 62% raw = 100% displayed
   623→# Formula: display_pct = (raw_pct * 100) / threshold
   624→if [ "$COMPACTION_THRESHOLD" -lt 100 ] && [ "$COMPACTION_THRESHOLD" -gt 0 ]; then
   625→    display_pct=$((raw_pct * 100 / COMPACTION_THRESHOLD))
   626→    # Cap at 100% (could exceed if past compaction point)
   627→    [ "$display_pct" -gt 100 ] && display_pct=100
   628→else
   629→    display_pct="$raw_pct"
   630→fi
   631→
   632→# Color based on scaled percentage (same thresholds work for scaled 0-100%)
   633→if [ "$display_pct" -ge 80 ]; then
   634→    pct_color="$ROSE"                  # Red: 80%+ - getting full
   635→elif [ "$display_pct" -ge 60 ]; then
   636→    pct_color='\033[38;2;251;146;60m'  # Orange: 60-80%
   637→elif [ "$display_pct" -ge 40 ]; then
   638→    pct_color='\033[38;2;251;191;36m'  # Yellow: 40-60%
   639→else
   640→    pct_color="$EMERALD"               # Green: <40%
   641→fi
   642→
   643→# Calculate bar width to match statusline content width (72 chars)
   644→bar_width=$(calc_bar_width "$MODE")
   645→
   646→case "$MODE" in
   647→    nano)
   648→        bar=$(render_context_bar $bar_width $display_pct)
   649→        printf "${CTX_PRIMARY}◉${RESET} ${bar} ${pct_color}${display_pct}%%${RESET}\n"
   650→        ;;
   651→    micro)
   652→        bar=$(render_context_bar $bar_width $display_pct)
   653→        printf "${CTX_PRIMARY}◉${RESET} ${bar} ${pct_color}${display_pct}%%${RESET}\n"
   654→        ;;
   655→    mini)
   656→        bar=$(render_context_bar $bar_width $display_pct)
   657→        printf "${CTX_PRIMARY}◉${RESET} ${CTX_SECONDARY}CONTEXT:${RESET} ${bar} ${pct_color}${display_pct}%%${RESET}\n"
   658→        ;;
   659→    normal)
   660→        bar=$(render_context_bar $bar_width $display_pct)
   661→        printf "${CTX_PRIMARY}◉${RESET} ${CTX_SECONDARY}CONTEXT:${RESET} ${bar} ${pct_color}${display_pct}%%${RESET}\n"
   662→        ;;
   663→esac
   664→printf "${SLATE_600}────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────${RESET}\n"
   665→
   666→# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
   667→# LINE 4: PWD & GIT STATUS
   668→# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
   669→# NOTE: is_git_repo, branch, stash_count, modified, staged, untracked, total_changed,
   670→#       ahead, behind, last_commit_epoch are populated by PARALLEL PREFETCH
   671→
   672→# Calculate age display from prefetched last_commit_epoch
   673→if [ "$is_git_repo" = "true" ] && [ -n "$last_commit_epoch" ]; then
   674→    now_epoch=$(date +%s)
   675→    age_seconds=$((now_epoch - last_commit_epoch))
   676→    age_minutes=$((age_seconds / 60))
   677→    age_hours=$((age_seconds / 3600))
   678→    age_days=$((age_seconds / 86400))
   679→
   680→    if   [ "$age_minutes" -lt 1 ];  then age_display="now";         age_color="$GIT_AGE_FRESH"
   681→    elif [ "$age_hours" -lt 1 ];    then age_display="${age_minutes}m"; age_color="$GIT_AGE_FRESH"
   682→    elif [ "$age_hours" -lt 24 ];   then age_display="${age_hours}h";   age_color="$GIT_AGE_RECENT"
   683→    elif [ "$age_days" -lt 7 ];     then age_display="${age_days}d";    age_color="$GIT_AGE_STALE"
   684→    else age_display="${age_days}d"; age_color="$GIT_AGE_OLD"
   685→    fi
   686→
   687→    # Status indicator
   688→    [ "$total_changed" -gt 0 ] || [ "$untracked" -gt 0 ] && git_status_icon="*" || git_status_icon="✓"
   689→fi
   690→
   691→# Always output PWD line, with git info if available
   692→case "$MODE" in
   693→    nano)
   694→        printf "${GIT_PRIMARY}◈${RESET} ${GIT_DIR}${dir_name}${RESET}"
   695→        [ "$is_git_repo" = true ] && printf " ${GIT_VALUE}${branch}${RESET} " && {
   696→            [ "$git_status_icon" = "✓" ] && printf "${GIT_CLEAN}✓${RESET}" || printf "${GIT_MODIFIED}*${total_changed}${RESET}"
   697→        }
   698→        printf "\n"
   699→        ;;
   700→    micro)
   701→        printf "${GIT_PRIMARY}◈${RESET} ${GIT_DIR}${dir_name}${RESET}"
   702→        if [ "$is_git_repo" = true ]; then
   703→            printf " ${GIT_VALUE}${branch}${RESET}"
   704→            [ -n "$age_display" ] && printf " ${age_color}${age_display}${RESET}"
   705→            printf " "
   706→            [ "$git_status_icon" = "✓" ] && printf "${GIT_CLEAN}${git_status_icon}${RESET}" || printf "${GIT_MODIFIED}${git_status_icon}${total_changed}${RESET}"
   707→        fi
   708→        printf "\n"
   709→        ;;
   710→    mini)
   711→        printf "${GIT_PRIMARY}◈${RESET} ${GIT_DIR}${dir_name}${RESET}"
   712→        if [ "$is_git_repo" = true ]; then
   713→            printf " ${SLATE_600}│${RESET} ${GIT_VALUE}${branch}${RESET}"
   714→            [ -n "$age_display" ] && printf " ${SLATE_600}│${RESET} ${age_color}${age_display}${RESET}"
   715→            printf " ${SLATE_600}│${RESET} "
   716→            if [ "$git_status_icon" = "✓" ]; then
   717→                printf "${GIT_CLEAN}${git_status_icon}${RESET}"
   718→            else
   719→                printf "${GIT_MODIFIED}${git_status_icon}${total_changed}${RESET}"
   720→                [ "$untracked" -gt 0 ] && printf " ${GIT_ADDED}+${untracked}${RESET}"
   721→            fi
   722→        fi
   723→        printf "\n"
   724→        ;;
   725→    normal)
   726→        printf "${GIT_PRIMARY}◈${RESET} ${GIT_PRIMARY}PWD:${RESET} ${GIT_DIR}${dir_name}${RESET}"
   727→        if [ "$is_git_repo" = true ]; then
   728→            printf " ${SLATE_600}│${RESET} ${GIT_PRIMARY}Branch:${RESET} ${GIT_VALUE}${branch}${RESET}"
   729→            [ -n "$age_display" ] && printf " ${SLATE_600}│${RESET} ${GIT_PRIMARY}Age:${RESET} ${age_color}${age_display}${RESET}"
   730→            [ "$stash_count" -gt 0 ] && printf " ${SLATE_600}│${RESET} ${GIT_PRIMARY}Stash:${RESET} ${GIT_STASH}${stash_count}${RESET}"
   731→            if [ "$total_changed" -gt 0 ] || [ "$untracked" -gt 0 ]; then
   732→                printf " ${SLATE_600}│${RESET} "
   733→                [ "$total_changed" -gt 0 ] && printf "${GIT_PRIMARY}Mod:${RESET} ${GIT_MODIFIED}${total_changed}${RESET}"
   734→                [ "$untracked" -gt 0 ] && { [ "$total_changed" -gt 0 ] && printf " "; printf "${GIT_PRIMARY}New:${RESET} ${GIT_ADDED}${untracked}${RESET}"; }
   735→            else
   736→                printf " ${SLATE_600}│${RESET} ${GIT_CLEAN}✓ clean${RESET}"
   737→            fi
   738→            if [ "$ahead" -gt 0 ] || [ "$behind" -gt 0 ]; then
   739→                printf " ${SLATE_600}│${RESET} ${GIT_PRIMARY}Sync:${RESET} "
   740→                [ "$ahead" -gt 0 ] && printf "${GIT_CLEAN}↑${ahead}${RESET}"
   741→                [ "$behind" -gt 0 ] && printf "${GIT_STASH}↓${behind}${RESET}"
   742→            fi
   743→        fi
   744→        printf "\n"
   745→        ;;
   746→esac
   747→printf "${SLATE_600}────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────${RESET}\n"
   748→
   749→# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
   750→# LINE 5: MEMORY
   751→# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
   752→
   753→case "$MODE" in
   754→    nano)
   755→        printf "${LEARN_PRIMARY}◎${RESET} ${LEARN_WORK}📁${RESET}${SLATE_300}${work_count}${RESET} ${LEARN_SIGNALS}✦${RESET}${SLATE_300}${ratings_count}${RESET} ${LEARN_SESSIONS}⊕${RESET}${SLATE_300}${sessions_count}${RESET} ${LEARN_RESEARCH}◇${RESET}${SLATE_300}${research_count}${RESET}\n"
   756→        ;;
   757→    micro)
   758→        printf "${LEARN_PRIMARY}◎${RESET} ${LEARN_WORK}📁${RESET}${SLATE_300}${work_count}${RESET} ${LEARN_SIGNALS}✦${RESET}${SLATE_300}${ratings_count}${RESET} ${LEARN_SESSIONS}⊕${RESET}${SLATE_300}${sessions_count}${RESET} ${LEARN_RESEARCH}◇${RESET}${SLATE_300}${research_count}${RESET}\n"
   759→        ;;
   760→    mini)
   761→        printf "${LEARN_PRIMARY}◎${RESET} ${LEARN_SECONDARY}MEMORY:${RESET} "
   762→        printf "${LEARN_WORK}📁${RESET}${SLATE_300}${work_count}${RESET} "
   763→        printf "${SLATE_600}│${RESET} ${LEARN_SIGNALS}✦${RESET}${SLATE_300}${ratings_count}${RESET} "
   764→        printf "${SLATE_600}│${RESET} ${LEARN_SESSIONS}⊕${RESET}${SLATE_300}${sessions_count}${RESET} "
   765→        printf "${SLATE_600}│${RESET} ${LEARN_RESEARCH}◇${RESET}${SLATE_300}${research_count}${RESET}\n"
   766→        ;;
   767→    normal)
   768→        printf "${LEARN_PRIMARY}◎${RESET} ${LEARN_SECONDARY}MEMORY:${RESET} "
   769→        printf "${LEARN_WORK}📁${RESET}${SLATE_300}${work_count}${RESET} ${LEARN_WORK}Work${RESET} "
   770→        printf "${SLATE_600}│${RESET} ${LEARN_SIGNALS}✦${RESET}${SLATE_300}${ratings_count}${RESET} ${LEARN_SIGNALS}Ratings${RESET} "
   771→        printf "${SLATE_600}│${RESET} ${LEARN_SESSIONS}⊕${RESET}${SLATE_300}${sessions_count}${RESET} ${LEARN_SESSIONS}Sessions${RESET} "
   772→        printf "${SLATE_600}│${RESET} ${LEARN_RESEARCH}◇${RESET}${SLATE_300}${research_count}${RESET} ${LEARN_RESEARCH}Research${RESET}\n"
   773→        ;;
   774→esac
   775→printf "${SLATE_600}────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────${RESET}\n"
   776→
   777→# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
   778→# LINE 6: LEARNING (with sparklines in normal mode)
   779→# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
   780→
   781→LEARNING_CACHE="$PAI_DIR/MEMORY/STATE/learning-cache.sh"
   782→LEARNING_CACHE_TTL=30  # seconds
   783→
   784→if [ -f "$RATINGS_FILE" ] && [ -s "$RATINGS_FILE" ]; then
   785→    now=$(date +%s)
   786→
   787→    # Check cache validity (by mtime and ratings file mtime)
   788→    cache_valid=false
   789→    if [ -f "$LEARNING_CACHE" ]; then
   790→        cache_mtime=$(stat -f %m "$LEARNING_CACHE" 2>/dev/null || echo 0)
   791→        ratings_mtime=$(stat -f %m "$RATINGS_FILE" 2>/dev/null || echo 0)
   792→        cache_age=$((now - cache_mtime))
   793→        # Cache valid if: cache newer than ratings AND cache age < TTL
   794→        if [ "$cache_mtime" -gt "$ratings_mtime" ] && [ "$cache_age" -lt "$LEARNING_CACHE_TTL" ]; then
   795→            cache_valid=true
   796→        fi
   797→    fi
   798→
   799→    if [ "$cache_valid" = true ]; then
   800→        # Use cached values
   801→        source "$LEARNING_CACHE"
   802→    else
   803→        # Compute fresh and cache
   804→        eval "$(jq -rs --argjson now "$now" '
   805→      # Parse ISO timestamp to epoch (handles timezone offsets)
   806→      def to_epoch:
   807→        (capture("(?<sign>[-+])(?<h>[0-9]{2}):(?<m>[0-9]{2})$") // {sign: "+", h: "00", m: "00"}) as $tz |
   808→        gsub("[-+][0-9]{2}:[0-9]{2}$"; "Z") | gsub("\\.[0-9]+"; "") | fromdateiso8601 |
   809→        . + (if $tz.sign == "-" then 1 else -1 end) * (($tz.h | tonumber) * 3600 + ($tz.m | tonumber) * 60);
   810→
   811→      # Filter valid ratings and add epoch
   812→      [.[] | select(.rating != null) | . + {epoch: (.timestamp | to_epoch)}] |
   813→
   814→      # Time boundaries
   815→      ($now - 900) as $q15_start | ($now - 3600) as $hour_start | ($now - 86400) as $today_start |
   816→      ($now - 604800) as $week_start | ($now - 2592000) as $month_start |
   817→
   818→      # Calculate averages
   819→      (map(select(.epoch >= $q15_start) | .rating) | if length > 0 then (add / length | . * 10 | floor / 10 | tostring) else "—" end) as $q15_avg |
   820→      (map(select(.epoch >= $hour_start) | .rating) | if length > 0 then (add / length | . * 10 | floor / 10 | tostring) else "—" end) as $hour_avg |
   821→      (map(select(.epoch >= $today_start) | .rating) | if length > 0 then (add / length | . * 10 | floor / 10 | tostring) else "—" end) as $today_avg |
   822→      (map(select(.epoch >= $week_start) | .rating) | if length > 0 then (add / length | . * 10 | floor / 10 | tostring) else "—" end) as $week_avg |
   823→      (map(select(.epoch >= $month_start) | .rating) | if length > 0 then (add / length | . * 10 | floor / 10 | tostring) else "—" end) as $month_avg |
   824→      (map(.rating) | if length > 0 then (add / length | . * 10 | floor / 10 | tostring) else "—" end) as $all_avg |
   825→
   826→      # Sparkline: diverging from 5, symmetric heights, color = direction
   827→      def to_bar:
   828→        floor |
   829→        if . >= 10 then "\u001b[38;2;34;197;94m▅\u001b[0m"      # brightest green
   830→        elif . >= 9 then "\u001b[38;2;74;222;128m▅\u001b[0m"    # green
   831→        elif . >= 8 then "\u001b[38;2;134;239;172m▄\u001b[0m"   # light green
   832→        elif . >= 7 then "\u001b[38;2;59;130;246m▃\u001b[0m"    # dark blue
   833→        elif . >= 6 then "\u001b[38;2;96;165;250m▂\u001b[0m"    # blue
   834→        elif . >= 5 then "\u001b[38;2;253;224;71m▁\u001b[0m"    # yellow baseline
   835→        elif . >= 4 then "\u001b[38;2;253;186;116m▂\u001b[0m"   # light orange
   836→        elif . >= 3 then "\u001b[38;2;251;146;60m▃\u001b[0m"    # orange
   837→        elif . >= 2 then "\u001b[38;2;248;113;113m▄\u001b[0m"   # light red
   838→        else "\u001b[38;2;239;68;68m▅\u001b[0m" end;            # red
   839→
   840→      def make_sparkline($period_start):
   841→        . as $all | ($now - $period_start) as $dur | ($dur / 58) as $sz |
   842→        [range(58) | . as $i | ($period_start + ($i * $sz)) as $s | ($s + $sz) as $e |
   843→          [$all[] | select(.epoch >= $s and .epoch < $e) | .rating] |
   844→          if length == 0 then "\u001b[38;2;45;50;60m \u001b[0m" else (add / length) | to_bar end
   845→        ] | join("");
   846→
   847→      (make_sparkline($q15_start)) as $q15_sparkline |
   848→      (make_sparkline($hour_start)) as $hour_sparkline |
   849→      (make_sparkline($today_start)) as $day_sparkline |
   850→      (make_sparkline($week_start)) as $week_sparkline |
   851→      (make_sparkline($month_start)) as $month_sparkline |
   852→
   853→      # Trend calculation helper
   854→      def calc_trend($data):
   855→        if ($data | length) >= 2 then
   856→          (($data | length) / 2 | floor) as $half |
   857→          ($data[-$half:] | add / length) as $recent |
   858→          ($data[:$half] | add / length) as $older |
   859→          ($recent - $older) | if . > 0.5 then "up" elif . < -0.5 then "down" else "stable" end
   860→        else "stable" end;
   861→
   862→      # Friendly summary helper (8 words max)
   863→      def friendly_summary($avg; $trend; $period):
   864→        if $avg == "—" then "No data yet for \($period)"
   865→        elif ($avg | tonumber) >= 8 then
   866→          if $trend == "up" then "Excellent and improving" elif $trend == "down" then "Great but cooling slightly" else "Smooth sailing, all good" end
   867→        elif ($avg | tonumber) >= 6 then
   868→          if $trend == "up" then "Good and getting better" elif $trend == "down" then "Okay but trending down" else "Solid, steady performance" end
   869→        elif ($avg | tonumber) >= 4 then
   870→          if $trend == "up" then "Recovering, headed right direction" elif $trend == "down" then "Needs attention, declining" else "Mixed results, room to improve" end
   871→        else
   872→          if $trend == "up" then "Rough but improving now" elif $trend == "down" then "Struggling, needs focus" else "Challenging period, stay sharp" end
   873→        end;
   874→
   875→      # Hour and day trends
   876→      ([.[] | select(.epoch >= $hour_start) | .rating]) as $hour_data |
   877→      ([.[] | select(.epoch >= $today_start) | .rating]) as $day_data |
   878→      (calc_trend($hour_data)) as $hour_trend |
   879→      (calc_trend($day_data)) as $day_trend |
   880→
   881→      # Generate friendly summaries
   882→      (friendly_summary($hour_avg; $hour_trend; "hour")) as $hour_summary |
   883→      (friendly_summary($today_avg; $day_trend; "day")) as $day_summary |
   884→
   885→      # Overall trend
   886→      length as $total |
   887→      (if $total >= 4 then
   888→        (($total / 2) | floor) as $half |
   889→        (.[- $half:] | map(.rating) | add / length) as $recent |
   890→        (.[:$half] | map(.rating) | add / length) as $older |
   891→        ($recent - $older) | if . > 0.3 then "up" elif . < -0.3 then "down" else "stable" end
   892→      else "stable" end) as $trend |
   893→
   894→      (last | .rating | tostring) as $latest |
   895→      (last | .source // "explicit") as $latest_source |
   896→
   897→      "latest=\($latest | @sh)\nlatest_source=\($latest_source | @sh)\n" +
   898→      "q15_avg=\($q15_avg | @sh)\nhour_avg=\($hour_avg | @sh)\ntoday_avg=\($today_avg | @sh)\n" +
   899→      "week_avg=\($week_avg | @sh)\nmonth_avg=\($month_avg | @sh)\nall_avg=\($all_avg | @sh)\n" +
   900→      "q15_sparkline=\($q15_sparkline | @sh)\nhour_sparkline=\($hour_sparkline | @sh)\nday_sparkline=\($day_sparkline | @sh)\n" +
   901→      "week_sparkline=\($week_sparkline | @sh)\nmonth_sparkline=\($month_sparkline | @sh)\n" +
   902→      "hour_trend=\($hour_trend | @sh)\nday_trend=\($day_trend | @sh)\n" +
   903→      "hour_summary=\($hour_summary | @sh)\nday_summary=\($day_summary | @sh)\n" +
   904→      "trend=\($trend | @sh)\ntotal_count=\($total)"
   905→    ' "$RATINGS_FILE" 2>/dev/null)"
   906→
   907→        # Save to cache for next time
   908→        cat > "$LEARNING_CACHE" << CACHE_EOF
   909→latest='$latest'
   910→latest_source='$latest_source'
   911→q15_avg='$q15_avg'
   912→hour_avg='$hour_avg'
   913→today_avg='$today_avg'
   914→week_avg='$week_avg'
   915→month_avg='$month_avg'
   916→all_avg='$all_avg'
   917→q15_sparkline='$q15_sparkline'
   918→hour_sparkline='$hour_sparkline'
   919→day_sparkline='$day_sparkline'
   920→week_sparkline='$week_sparkline'
   921→month_sparkline='$month_sparkline'
   922→hour_trend='$hour_trend'
   923→day_trend='$day_trend'
   924→hour_summary='$hour_summary'
   925→day_summary='$day_summary'
   926→trend='$trend'
   927→total_count=$total_count
   928→CACHE_EOF
   929→    fi  # end cache computation
   930→
   931→    if [ "$total_count" -gt 0 ] 2>/dev/null; then
   932→        # Trend icon/color
   933→        case "$trend" in
   934→            up)   trend_icon="↗"; trend_color="$EMERALD" ;;
   935→            down) trend_icon="↘"; trend_color="$ROSE" ;;
   936→            *)    trend_icon="→"; trend_color="$SLATE_400" ;;
   937→        esac
   938→
   939→        # Get colors
   940→        [ "$q15_avg" != "—" ] && pulse_base="$q15_avg" || { [ "$hour_avg" != "—" ] && pulse_base="$hour_avg" || { [ "$today_avg" != "—" ] && pulse_base="$today_avg" || pulse_base="$all_avg"; }; }
   941→        PULSE_COLOR=$(get_rating_color "$pulse_base")
   942→        LATEST_COLOR=$(get_rating_color "${latest:-5}")
   943→        Q15_COLOR=$(get_rating_color "${q15_avg:-5}")
   944→        HOUR_COLOR=$(get_rating_color "${hour_avg:-5}")
   945→        TODAY_COLOR=$(get_rating_color "${today_avg:-5}")
   946→        WEEK_COLOR=$(get_rating_color "${week_avg:-5}")
   947→        MONTH_COLOR=$(get_rating_color "${month_avg:-5}")
   948→        ALL_COLOR=$(get_rating_color "$all_avg")
   949→
   950→        [ "$latest_source" = "explicit" ] && src_label="EXP" || src_label="IMP"
   951→
   952→        case "$MODE" in
   953→            nano)
   954→                printf "${LEARN_LABEL}✿${RESET} ${LATEST_COLOR}${latest}${RESET} ${SIGNAL_PERIOD}1d:${RESET} ${TODAY_COLOR}${today_avg}${RESET}\n"
   955→                ;;
   956→            micro)
   957→                printf "${LEARN_LABEL}✿${RESET} ${LATEST_COLOR}${latest}${RESET} ${SIGNAL_PERIOD}1h:${RESET} ${HOUR_COLOR}${hour_avg}${RESET} ${SIGNAL_PERIOD}1d:${RESET} ${TODAY_COLOR}${today_avg}${RESET} ${SIGNAL_PERIOD}1w:${RESET} ${WEEK_COLOR}${week_avg}${RESET}\n"
   958→                ;;
   959→            mini)
   960→                printf "${LEARN_LABEL}✿${RESET} ${LEARN_LABEL}LEARNING:${RESET} ${SLATE_600}│${RESET} "
   961→                printf "${LATEST_COLOR}${latest}${RESET} "
   962→                printf "${SIGNAL_PERIOD}1h:${RESET} ${HOUR_COLOR}${hour_avg}${RESET} "
   963→                printf "${SIGNAL_PERIOD}1d:${RESET} ${TODAY_COLOR}${today_avg}${RESET} "
   964→                printf "${SIGNAL_PERIOD}1w:${RESET} ${WEEK_COLOR}${week_avg}${RESET}\n"
   965→                ;;
   966→            normal)
   967→                printf "${LEARN_LABEL}✿${RESET} ${LEARN_LABEL}LEARNING:${RESET} ${SLATE_600}│${RESET} "
   968→                printf "${LATEST_COLOR}${latest}${RESET}${SLATE_500}${src_label}${RESET} ${SLATE_600}│${RESET} "
   969→                printf "${SIGNAL_PERIOD}15m:${RESET} ${Q15_COLOR}${q15_avg}${RESET} "
   970→                printf "${SIGNAL_PERIOD}60m:${RESET} ${HOUR_COLOR}${hour_avg}${RESET} "
   971→                printf "${SIGNAL_PERIOD}1d:${RESET} ${TODAY_COLOR}${today_avg}${RESET} "
   972→                printf "${SIGNAL_PERIOD}1w:${RESET} ${WEEK_COLOR}${week_avg}${RESET} "
   973→                printf "${SIGNAL_PERIOD}1mo:${RESET} ${MONTH_COLOR}${month_avg}${RESET}\n"
   974→
   975→                # Sparklines (condensed, no blank lines)
   976→                printf "   ${SLATE_600}├─${RESET} ${SIGNAL_PERIOD}%-5s${RESET} %s\n" "15m:" "$q15_sparkline"
   977→                printf "   ${SLATE_600}├─${RESET} ${SIGNAL_PERIOD}%-5s${RESET} %s\n" "60m:" "$hour_sparkline"
   978→                printf "   ${SLATE_600}├─${RESET} ${SIGNAL_PERIOD}%-5s${RESET} %s\n" "1d:" "$day_sparkline"
   979→                printf "   ${SLATE_600}├─${RESET} ${SIGNAL_PERIOD}%-5s${RESET} %s\n" "1w:" "$week_sparkline"
   980→                printf "   ${SLATE_600}└─${RESET} ${SIGNAL_PERIOD}%-5s${RESET} %s\n" "1mo:" "$month_sparkline"
   981→                ;;
   982→        esac
   983→    else
   984→        printf "${LEARN_LABEL}✿${RESET} ${LEARN_LABEL}LEARNING:${RESET}\n"
   985→        printf "  ${SLATE_500}No ratings yet${RESET}\n"
   986→    fi
   987→else
   988→    printf "${LEARN_LABEL}✿${RESET} ${LEARN_LABEL}LEARNING:${RESET}\n"
   989→    printf "  ${SLATE_500}No ratings yet${RESET}\n"
   990→fi
   991→
   992→# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
   993→# LINE 7: QUOTE (normal mode only)
   994→# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
   995→
   996→if [ "$MODE" = "normal" ]; then
   997→    printf "${SLATE_600}────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────${RESET}\n"
   998→
   999→    # Refresh quote if stale (>30s)
  1000→    quote_age=$(($(date +%s) - $(stat -f %m "$QUOTE_CACHE" 2>/dev/null || echo 0)))
  1001→    if [ "$quote_age" -gt 30 ] || [ ! -f "$QUOTE_CACHE" ]; then
  1002→        if [ -n "${ZENQUOTES_API_KEY:-}" ]; then
  1003→            new_quote=$(curl -s --max-time 1 "https://zenquotes.io/api/random/${ZENQUOTES_API_KEY}" 2>/dev/null | \
  1004→                jq -r '.[0] | select(.q | length < 80) | .q + "|" + .a' 2>/dev/null)
  1005→            [ -n "$new_quote" ] && [ "$new_quote" != "null" ] && echo "$new_quote" > "$QUOTE_CACHE"
  1006→        fi
  1007→    fi
  1008→
  1009→    if [ -f "$QUOTE_CACHE" ]; then
  1010→        IFS='|' read -r quote_text quote_author < "$QUOTE_CACHE"
  1011→        author_suffix="\" —${quote_author}"
  1012→        author_len=${#author_suffix}
  1013→        quote_len=${#quote_text}
  1014→        max_line=72
  1015→
  1016→        # Full display: ✦ "quote text" —Author
  1017→        full_len=$((quote_len + author_len + 4))  # 4 for ✦ "
  1018→
  1019→        if [ "$full_len" -le "$max_line" ]; then
  1020→            # Fits on one line
  1021→            printf "${QUOTE_PRIMARY}✦${RESET} ${SLATE_400}\"${quote_text}\"${RESET} ${QUOTE_AUTHOR}—${quote_author}${RESET}\n"
  1022→        else
  1023→            # Need to wrap - target ~10 words (55-60 chars) on first line
  1024→            # Line 1 gets: "✦ \"" (4) + text
  1025→            line1_text_max=60  # ~10 words worth
  1026→
  1027→            # Only wrap if there's substantial content left for line 2
  1028→            min_line2=12
  1029→
  1030→            # Target: put ~60 chars on line 1
  1031→            target_line1=$line1_text_max
  1032→            [ "$target_line1" -gt "$quote_len" ] && target_line1=$((quote_len - min_line2))
  1033→
  1034→            # Find word boundary near target
  1035→            first_part="${quote_text:0:$target_line1}"
  1036→            remaining="${quote_text:$target_line1}"
  1037→
  1038→            # If we're not at a space, find the last space in first_part
  1039→            if [ -n "$remaining" ] && [ "${remaining:0:1}" != " " ]; then
  1040→                # Find last space position
  1041→                temp="$first_part"
  1042→                last_space_pos=0
  1043→                pos=0
  1044→                while [ $pos -lt ${#temp} ]; do
  1045→                    [ "${temp:$pos:1}" = " " ] && last_space_pos=$pos
  1046→                    pos=$((pos + 1))
  1047→                done
  1048→                if [ $last_space_pos -gt 10 ]; then
  1049→                    first_part="${quote_text:0:$last_space_pos}"
  1050→                fi
  1051→            fi
  1052→
  1053→            second_part="${quote_text:${#first_part}}"
  1054→            second_part="${second_part# }"  # trim leading space
  1055→
  1056→            # Only wrap if second part is substantial (more than just a few words)
  1057→            if [ ${#second_part} -lt 10 ]; then
  1058→                # Too little for line 2, just print on one line (may overflow slightly)
  1059→                printf "${QUOTE_PRIMARY}✦${RESET} ${SLATE_400}\"${quote_text}\"${RESET} ${QUOTE_AUTHOR}—${quote_author}${RESET}\n"
  1060→            else
  1061→                printf "${QUOTE_PRIMARY}✦${RESET} ${SLATE_400}\"${first_part}${RESET}\n"
  1062→                printf "  ${SLATE_400}${second_part}\"${RESET} ${QUOTE_AUTHOR}—${quote_author}${RESET}\n"
  1063→            fi
  1064→        fi
  1065→    fi
  1066→fi

content.content:
1→#!/bin/bash
     2→# Start PAI Voice Server V2 (Qwen3-TTS, uv-based)
     3→
     4→SCRIPT_DIR="$( cd "$( dirname "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}" )" && pwd )"
     5→PLIST="$HOME/Library/LaunchAgents/com.pai.voice-server.plist"
     6→PORT=8888
     7→
     8→echo "Starting PAI Voice Server V2..."
     9→
    10→# Check if already running
    11→if lsof -i :$PORT > /dev/null 2>&1; then
    12→    echo "Server already running on port $PORT"
    13→    echo "Use ./restart.sh to restart"
    14→    exit 0
    15→fi
    16→
    17→# Install updated plist
    18→sed "s|__HOME__|$HOME|g" "$SCRIPT_DIR/macos-service/com.pai.voice-server.plist" > "$PLIST"
    19→
    20→# Load LaunchAgent
    21→if [ -f "$PLIST" ]; then
    22→    launchctl load "$PLIST" 2>/dev/null || true
    23→    echo "LaunchAgent loaded"
    24→else
    25→    echo "LaunchAgent not found. Running directly with uv..."
    26→    cd "$SCRIPT_DIR"
    27→    uv run uvicorn server:app --host 127.0.0.1 --port $PORT &
    28→fi
    29→
    30→# Wait for startup
    31→echo "Waiting for server to start..."
    32→sleep 3
    33→
    34→# Check health
    35→if curl -s -f http://localhost:$PORT/health > /dev/null 2>&1; then
    36→    echo ""
    37→    echo "PAI Voice Server V2 is running on port $PORT"
    38→    echo "Health: http://localhost:$PORT/health"
    39→else
    40→    echo ""
    41→    echo "Server may still be starting (model loading takes time)"
    42→    echo "Check logs: tail -f $SCRIPT_DIR/logs/voice-server.log"
    43→fi
    44→

content.content:
1→/**
     2→ * Shared Learning Utilities
     3→ *
     4→ * Categorization logic for learnings across the hook system.
     5→ * Used by: ExplicitRatingCapture, ImplicitSentimentCapture, ResponseCapture
     6→ */
     7→
     8→/**
     9→ * Categorize learning as SYSTEM (tooling/infrastructure) or ALGORITHM (task execution)
    10→ *
    11→ * SYSTEM = hook failures, tooling issues, infrastructure problems, system errors
    12→ * ALGORITHM = task execution issues, approach errors, method improvements
    13→ *
    14→ * Check ALGORITHM first because user feedback about task execution is more valuable.
    15→ * Default to ALGORITHM since most learnings are about task quality, not infrastructure.
    16→ *
    17→ * @param content - The main content to analyze
    18→ * @param comment - Optional user comment to include in analysis
    19→ */
    20→export function getLearningCategory(content: string, comment?: string): 'SYSTEM' | 'ALGORITHM' {
    21→  const text = `${content} ${comment || ''}`.toLowerCase();
    22→
    23→  // ALGORITHM indicators - task execution/approach issues (check first)
    24→  const algorithmIndicators = [
    25→    /over.?engineer/,
    26→    /wrong approach/,
    27→    /should have asked/,
    28→    /didn't follow/,
    29→    /missed the point/,
    30→    /too complex/,
    31→    /didn't understand/,
    32→    /wrong direction/,
    33→    /not what i wanted/,
    34→    /approach|method|strategy|reasoning/
    35→  ];
    36→
    37→  // SYSTEM indicators - tooling/infrastructure issues
    38→  const systemIndicators = [
    39→    /hook|crash|broken/,
    40→    /tool|config|deploy|path/,
    41→    /import|module|file.*not.*found/,
    42→    /typescript|javascript|npm|bun/
    43→  ];
    44→
    45→  // Check ALGORITHM first (user feedback about approach is valuable)
    46→  for (const pattern of algorithmIndicators) {
    47→    if (pattern.test(text)) return 'ALGORITHM';
    48→  }
    49→
    50→  for (const pattern of systemIndicators) {
    51→    if (pattern.test(text)) return 'SYSTEM';
    52→  }
    53→
    54→  // Default: learnings reflect task quality → ALGORITHM
    55→  return 'ALGORITHM';
    56→}
    57→
    58→/**
    59→ * Determine if a response represents a learning moment
    60→ */
    61→export function isLearningCapture(text: string, summary?: string, analysis?: string): boolean {
    62→  const learningIndicators = [
    63→    /problem|issue|bug|error|failed|broken/i,
    64→    /fixed|solved|resolved|discovered|realized|learned/i,
    65→    /troubleshoot|debug|investigate|root cause/i,
    66→    /lesson|takeaway|now we know|next time/i,
    67→  ];
    68→
    69→  const checkText = `${summary || ''} ${analysis || ''} ${text}`;
    70→
    71→  let indicatorCount = 0;
    72→  for (const pattern of learningIndicators) {
    73→    if (pattern.test(checkText)) {
    74→      indicatorCount++;
    75→    }
    76→  }
    77→
    78→  // If 2+ learning indicators, consider it a learning
    79→  return indicatorCount >= 2;
    80→}
    81→

     4→set -e

     6→SCRIPT_DIR="$( cd "$( dirname "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}" )" && pwd )"
     7→PLIST_SRC="$SCRIPT_DIR/macos-service/com.pai.qwen3-voice-server.plist"
     8→PLIST_DST="$HOME/Library/LaunchAgents/com.pai.qwen3-voice-server.plist"
     9→
    10→echo "=================================================="
    11→echo "  Qwen3-TTS Voice Server Installation"
    12→echo "=================================================="
    13→
    14→# Check uv
    15→echo ""
    16→echo "1. Checking uv..."
    17→if ! command -v uv &> /dev/null; then
    18→    echo "   ERROR: uv not found."
    19→    echo "   Install with: curl -LsSf https://astral.sh/uv/install.sh | sh"
    20→    exit 1
    21→fi
    22→echo "   Using: $(uv --version)"
    23→
    24→# Initialize uv project if needed
    25→echo ""
    26→echo "2. Setting up uv environment..."
    27→cd "$SCRIPT_DIR"
    28→
    29→if [ ! -f "pyproject.toml" ]; then
    30→    echo "   Creating pyproject.toml..."
    31→    uv init --no-readme --no-git
    32→fi
    33→
    34→# Sync dependencies
    35→echo ""
    36→echo "3. Installing dependencies with uv..."
    37→uv sync
    38→echo "   Dependencies installed"
    39→
    40→# Create directories
    41→echo ""
    42→echo "4. Creating directories..."
    43→mkdir -p "$SCRIPT_DIR/voices/prompts"
    44→mkdir -p "$SCRIPT_DIR/logs"
    45→mkdir -p "$SCRIPT_DIR/reference_audio"
    46→mkdir -p /tmp/qwen3-voice
    47→echo "   Directories ready"
    48→
    49→# Initialize voice index

    51→echo "5. Initializing voice storage..."
    52→INDEX_FILE="$SCRIPT_DIR/voices/index.json"
    53→if [ ! -f "$INDEX_FILE" ]; then
    54→    echo '{"prompts": {}}' > "$INDEX_FILE"
    55→    echo "   Created voice index"
    56→else

    58→fi
    59→
    60→# Install LaunchAgent

    62→echo "6. Installing LaunchAgent..."
    63→mkdir -p "$HOME/Library/LaunchAgents"
    64→
    65→# Update plist with actual home directory
    66→sed "s|__HOME__|$HOME|g" "$PLIST_SRC" > "$PLIST_DST"
    67→echo "   Installed: $PLIST_DST"
    68→
    69→# Make scripts executable
    70→echo ""
    71→echo "7. Setting permissions..."
    72→chmod +x "$SCRIPT_DIR"/*.sh
    73→echo "   Scripts are executable"
    74→
    75→echo ""
    76→echo "=================================================="
    77→echo "  Installation Complete!"
    78→echo "=================================================="

1→# PAI Installation Guide
     2→
     3→Welcome to PAI (Personal AI Infrastructure) - a framework for maximizing AI assistance across all domains of your life.
     4→
     5→## Prerequisites
     6→
     7→Before installing, ensure you have:
     8→
     9→1. **Bun** - JavaScript/TypeScript runtime
    10→   ```bash
    11→   curl -fsSL https://bun.sh/install | bash
    12→   ```
    13→
    14→2. **Claude Code** - Anthropic's CLI for Claude
    15→   ```bash
    16→   npm install -g @anthropic-ai/claude-code
    17→   # or
    18→   brew install claude-code
    19→   ```
    20→
    21→3. **API Keys** (optional but recommended)
    22→   - **ElevenLabs** - For voice synthesis ([elevenlabs.io](https://elevenlabs.io))
    23→   - **Anthropic** - Only needed if not using Claude Code subscription
    24→
    25→   API keys are stored in `${PAI_DIR}/.env`. See `.env.example` for template.
    26→
    27→---
    28→
    29→## Installation Methods
    30→
    31→### Method 1: Full Installation (Recommended)
    32→
    33→The complete PAI experience with all skills, hooks, and infrastructure.
    34→
    35→```bash
    36→# Clone or download the PAI release
    37→cd ~/.claude
    38→
    39→# Run the installation wizard
    40→bun run INSTALL.ts
    41→```
    42→
    43→The wizard will:
    44→- Detect any existing PAI installations
    45→- Ask for your name and preferences
    46→- Configure API keys (ElevenLabs, etc.)
    47→- Migrate personal content from backups
    48→- Validate the installation
    49→
    50→### Method 2: Pack Installation
    51→
    52→Install specific capabilities as modular packs:
    53→
    54→```bash
    55→# List available packs
    56→ls Packs/
    57→
    58→# Read a pack to see what it does
    59→cat Packs/pai-voice-system.md
    60→
    61→# Follow pack instructions to install specific capabilities
    62→```
    63→
    64→### Method 3: Just Use It
    65→
    66→PAI works out of the box:
    67→
    68→1. Copy the `.claude/` directory to your home folder
    69→2. Start Claude Code: `cd ~/.claude && claude`
    70→3. It works immediately with default settings
    71→4. Run `bun run INSTALL.ts` later to customize your name and voice
    72→
    73→---
    74→
    75→## Configuration
    76→
    77→### settings.json
    78→
    79→The main configuration file. Required fields:
    80→
    81→```json
    82→{
    83→  "paiVersion": "2.5",
    84→  "principal": {
    85→    "name": "Your Name",
    86→    "timezone": "America/Los_Angeles"
    87→  },
    88→  "daidentity": {
    89→    "name": "{DAIDENTITY.NAME}",
    90→    "fullName": "{DAIDENTITY.NAME} - Personal AI",
    91→    "voiceId": "your-elevenlabs-voice-id"
    92→  }
    93→}
    94→```
    95→
    96→**Note:** API keys go in `${PAI_DIR}/.env`, not in settings.json. See `.env.example` for the template.
    97→
    98→### Optional Configuration
    99→
   100→| Setting | Purpose | Default |
   101→|---------|---------|---------|
   102→| `techStack.runtime` | JavaScript runtime | `bun` |
   103→| `techStack.browser` | Preferred browser | `arc` |
   104→| `contextualNotifications` | Enable voice alerts | `true` |
   105→
   106→---
   107→
   108→## Migration from Existing Installation
   109→
   110→If you have an existing `~/.claude/` or `~/.claude-BACKUP/`, the wizard will:
   111→
   112→1. **Detect** your existing installation
   113→2. **Scan** for transferable content:
   114→   - settings.json (your identity, API keys)
   115→   - USER/ content (personal customizations)
   116→   - Personal skills (_ALLCAPS named)
   117→   - Agent configurations
   118→   - MEMORY/STATE (work in progress)
   119→   - Plans (in-progress plans)
   120→
   121→3. **Merge** intelligently:
   122→   - Preserves your personal data
   123→   - Updates system components
   124→   - Handles conflicts gracefully
   125→
   126→The wizard handles migration automatically when it detects an existing installation.
   127→
   128→---
   129→
   130→## Verification
   131→
   132→After installation, verify everything works:
   133→
   134→### Quick Test
   135→
   136→Start Claude Code in your PAI directory:
   137→
   138→```bash
   139→cd ~/.claude
   140→claude
   141→```
   142→
   143→Claude should greet you by name and display the PAI banner.
   144→
   145→---
   146→
   147→## Directory Structure
   148→
   149→After installation, your `~/.claude/` will contain:
   150→
   151→```
   152→.claude/
   153→├── settings.json          # Your configuration
   154→├── .env                   # API keys (ELEVENLABS_API_KEY, etc.)
   155→├── CLAUDE.md              # Entry point for Claude
   156→├── skills/                # Skill modules
   157→│   ├── PAI/               # Core system skill
   158→│   │   ├── USER/          # Your personal content
   159→│   │   └── SYSTEM/        # System documentation
   160→│   └── [OtherSkills]/     # Additional skills
   161→├── MEMORY/                # Session history & learnings
   162→├── hooks/                 # Lifecycle event handlers
   163→├── agents/                # Named agent configurations
   164→├── Plans/                 # Plan mode working files
   165→├── WORK/                  # Active work sessions
   166→├── tools/                 # CLI utilities
   167→└── bin/                   # Executable scripts
   168→```
   169→
   170→---
   171→
   172→## Troubleshooting
   173→
   174→### "settings.json not found"
   175→
   176→Run the installation wizard:
   177→```bash
   178→bun run INSTALL.ts
   179→```
   180→
   181→### "PAI skill not found"
   182→
   183→Your installation may be incomplete. Re-run the wizard:
   184→```bash
   185→bun run INSTALL.ts
   186→```
   187→
   188→### "Bun not installed"
   189→
   190→Install Bun:
   191→```bash
   192→curl -fsSL https://bun.sh/install | bash
   193→source ~/.bashrc  # or ~/.zshrc
   194→```
   195→
   196→### Voice not working
   197→
   198→1. Check ElevenLabs API key in `${PAI_DIR}/.env` (set `ELEVENLABS_API_KEY`)
   199→2. Verify voice server is running: `curl http://localhost:8888/health`
   200→3. Check voice ID is valid in `settings.json` (`daidentity.voiceId`)
   201→
   202→### Migration didn't find my data
   203→
   204→The wizard looks in these locations:
   205→- `~/.claude-BACKUP/`
   206→- `~/.claude-old/`
   207→
   208→If your backup is elsewhere:
   209→```bash
   210→# Manually copy before running wizard
   211→cp -r /path/to/your/backup ~/.claude-BACKUP
   212→bun run INSTALL.ts
   213→```
   214→
   215→---
   216→
   217→## Getting Help
   218→
   219→- **In Claude Code**: Ask "Help me with PAI" or "/help"
   220→- **Documentation**: See `skills/PAI/SKILL.md`
   221→- **GitHub**: [github.com/danielmiessler/PAI](https://github.com/danielmiessler/PAI)
   222→
   223→---
   224→
   225→## What's Next?
   226→
   227→After installation:
   228→
   229→1. **Explore skills**: Ask Claude "What skills do I have?"
   230→2. **Set up voice**: Configure ElevenLabs for voice responses
   231→3. **Create personal skills**: Add `_YOURSKILL` directories for private workflows
   232→4. **Learn the system**: Read `skills/PAI/SKILL.md` for full documentation
   233→
   234→Welcome to PAI!
   235→

<system-reminder>
Whenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. You CAN and SHOULD provide analysis of malware, what it is doing. But you MUST refuse to improve or augment the code. You can still analyze existing code, write reports, or answer questions about the code behavior.
</system-reminder>

content.content:
1→#!/usr/bin/env bun
     2→/**
     3→ * QuestionAnswered.hook.ts - Reset Tab After Question Answered (PostToolUse)
     4→ *
     5→ * PURPOSE:
     6→ * Resets the terminal tab from question state (teal) back to working state (orange)
     7→ * after the user answers an AskUserQuestion prompt. This bridges the gap where
     8→ * question answers don't trigger UserPromptSubmit (they're tool results, not prompts).
     9→ *
    10→ * TRIGGER: PostToolUse (matcher: AskUserQuestion)
    11→ *
    12→ * INPUT:
    13→ * - stdin: Hook input JSON with tool_input containing the user's answer
    14→ *
    15→ * OUTPUT:
    16→ * - stdout: None
    17→ * - stderr: Status message
    18→ * - exit(0): Always (non-blocking)
    19→ *
    20→ * SIDE EFFECTS:
    21→ * - Kitty remote control: Sets tab color to orange (#804000)
    22→ * - Kitty remote control: Sets tab title to working format
    23→ *
    24→ * INTER-HOOK RELATIONSHIPS:
    25→ * - DEPENDS ON: SetQuestionTab (runs before this, sets teal)
    26→ * - COORDINATES WITH: UpdateTabTitle (shares tab color scheme)
    27→ * - MUST RUN AFTER: SetQuestionTab (resets its teal color)
    28→ *
    29→ * TAB COLOR SCHEME (inactive tab only - active tab stays dark blue):
    30→ * - Dark teal (#085050): Waiting for user input (SetQuestionTab)
    31→ * - Dark orange (#804000): Actively working (this hook + UpdateTabTitle)
    32→ * - Dark purple (#1E0A3C): AI inference/thinking (UpdateTabTitle)
    33→ * - Dark blue (#002B80): Active tab always uses this
    34→ *
    35→ * ERROR HANDLING:
    36→ * - Kitty unavailable: Silent failure
    37→ */
    38→
    39→const TAB_WORKING_BG = '#804000';      // Dark orange - actively working
    40→const ACTIVE_TAB_BG = '#002B80';       // Dark blue - active tab always
    41→const ACTIVE_TEXT = '#FFFFFF';
    42→const INACTIVE_TEXT = '#A0A0A0';
    43→
    44→async function main() {
    45→  try {
    46→    // Set tab color: active stays dark blue, inactive shows orange
    47→    await Bun.$`kitten @ set-tab-color --self active_bg=${ACTIVE_TAB_BG} active_fg=${ACTIVE_TEXT} inactive_bg=${TAB_WORKING_BG} inactive_fg=${INACTIVE_TEXT}`.quiet();
    48→
    49→    // Set working title
    50→    await Bun.$`kitty @ set-tab-title "⚙️Processing answer…"`.quiet();
    51→
    52→    console.error('[QuestionAnswered] Tab reset to working state (orange on inactive only)');
    53→  } catch (error) {
    54→    // Silently fail if kitty remote control is not available
    55→    console.error('[QuestionAnswered] Kitty remote control unavailable');
    56→  }
    57→
    58→  process.exit(0);
    59→}
    60→
    61→main();
    62→

content.content:
1→# PAI Hook System
     2→
     3→> **Lifecycle event handlers that extend Claude Code with voice, memory, security, and observability.**
     4→
     5→This document is the authoritative reference for PAI's hook system. When modifying any hook, update both the hook's inline documentation AND this README.
     6→
     7→---
     8→
     9→## Table of Contents
    10→
    11→1. [Architecture Overview](#architecture-overview)
    12→2. [Hook Lifecycle Events](#hook-lifecycle-events)
    13→3. [Hook Registry](#hook-registry)
    14→4. [Inter-Hook Dependencies](#inter-hook-dependencies)
    15→5. [Data Flow Diagrams](#data-flow-diagrams)
    16→6. [Shared Libraries](#shared-libraries)
    17→7. [Configuration](#configuration)
    18→8. [Documentation Standards](#documentation-standards)
    19→9. [Maintenance Checklist](#maintenance-checklist)
    20→
    21→---
    22→
    23→## Architecture Overview
    24→
    25→Hooks are TypeScript scripts that execute at specific lifecycle events in Claude Code. They enable:
    26→
    27→- **Voice Feedback**: Spoken announcements of tasks and completions
    28→- **Memory Capture**: Session summaries, work tracking, learnings
    29→- **Security Validation**: Command filtering, path protection, prompt injection defense
    30→- **Observability**: Tab titles, sentiment tracking, ratings
    31→- **Context Injection**: Identity, preferences, format specifications
    32→
    33→### Design Principles
    34→
    35→1. **Non-blocking by default**: Hooks should not delay the user experience
    36→2. **Fail gracefully**: Errors in one hook must not crash the session
    37→3. **Single responsibility**: Each hook does one thing well
    38→4. **Orchestration over duplication**: Use StopOrchestrator for shared data needs
    39→
    40→### Execution Model
    41→
    42→```
    43→┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
    44→│                        Claude Code Session                          │
    45→├─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
    46→│                                                                     │
    47→│  SessionStart ──┬──► StartupGreeting (banner + stats)               │
    48→│                 ├──► LoadContext (PAI skill injection)             │
    49→│                 └──► CheckVersion (update notification)             │
    50→│                                                                     │
    51→│  UserPromptSubmit ──┬──► FormatEnforcer (response spec injection)   │
    52→│                     ├──► AutoWorkCreation (work directory setup)    │
    53→│                     ├──► ExplicitRatingCapture (1-10 ratings)       │
    54→│                     ├──► ImplicitSentimentCapture (mood detection)  │
    55→│                     └──► UpdateTabTitle (tab + voice announcement)  │
    56→│                                                                     │
    57→│  PreToolUse ──┬──► SecurityValidator (Bash/Edit/Write/Read)         │
    58→│               └──► SetQuestionTab (AskUserQuestion)                 │
    59→│                                                                     │
    60→│  SubagentStop ──► AgentOutputCapture (subagent results)             │
    61→│                                                                     │
    62→│  Stop ──► StopOrchestrator ──┬──► ResponseCapture                   │
    63→│                              ├──► TabTitleReset                     │
    64→│                              └──► VoiceCompletion                   │
    65→│                                                                     │
    66→│  SessionEnd ──┬──► WorkCompletionLearning (insight extraction)      │
    67→│               └──► SessionSummary (work directory completion)       │
    68→│                                                                     │
    69→└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
    70→```
    71→
    72→---
    73→
    74→## Hook Lifecycle Events
    75→
    76→| Event | When It Fires | Typical Use Cases |
    77→|-------|---------------|-------------------|
    78→| `SessionStart` | Session begins | Context loading, banner display, version check |
    79→| `UserPromptSubmit` | User sends a message | Format injection, work tracking, sentiment analysis |
    80→| `PreToolUse` | Before a tool executes | Security validation, UI state changes |
    81→| `SubagentStop` | Subagent completes | Capture subagent outputs for memory |
    82→| `Stop` | Claude responds | Voice feedback, tab updates, response capture |
    83→| `SessionEnd` | Session terminates | Summary generation, learning extraction |
    84→
    85→### Event Payload Structure
    86→
    87→All hooks receive JSON via stdin with event-specific fields:
    88→
    89→```typescript
    90→// Common fields
    91→interface BasePayload {
    92→  session_id: string;
    93→  transcript_path: string;
    94→  hook_event_name: string;
    95→}
    96→
    97→// UserPromptSubmit
    98→interface UserPromptPayload extends BasePayload {
    99→  prompt: string;
   100→}
   101→
   102→// PreToolUse
   103→interface PreToolUsePayload extends BasePayload {
   104→  tool_name: string;
   105→  tool_input: Record<string, any>;
   106→}
   107→
   108→// Stop
   109→interface StopPayload extends BasePayload {
   110→  stop_hook_active: boolean;
   111→}
   112→```
   113→
   114→---
   115→
   116→## Hook Registry
   117→
   118→### SessionStart Hooks
   119→
   120→| Hook | Purpose | Blocking | Dependencies |
   121→|------|---------|----------|--------------|
   122→| `StartupGreeting.hook.ts` | Display PAI banner with system stats | No | None |
   123→| `LoadContext.hook.ts` | Inject CORE skill into context | Yes (stdout) | `skills/PAI/SKILL.md` |
   124→| `CheckVersion.hook.ts` | Notify if CC update available | No | npm registry |
   125→
   126→### UserPromptSubmit Hooks
   127→
   128→| Hook | Purpose | Blocking | Dependencies |
   129→|------|---------|----------|--------------|
   130→| `FormatEnforcer.hook.ts` | Inject response format spec | Yes (stdout) | `USER/RESPONSEFORMAT.md` |
   131→| `AutoWorkCreation.hook.ts` | Create/update work directories | No | `MEMORY/STATE/current-work.json` |
   132→| `ExplicitRatingCapture.hook.ts` | Capture 1-10 ratings | No | `MEMORY/LEARNING/SIGNALS/ratings.jsonl` |
   133→| `ImplicitSentimentCapture.hook.ts` | Detect emotional sentiment | No | Inference API, `ratings.jsonl` |
   134→| `UpdateTabTitle.hook.ts` | Set tab title + voice announcement | No | Inference API, Voice Server |
   135→
   136→### PreToolUse Hooks
   137→
   138→| Hook | Purpose | Blocking | Dependencies |
   139→|------|---------|----------|--------------|
   140→| `SecurityValidator.hook.ts` | Validate Bash/Edit/Write/Read | Yes (decision) | `patterns.yaml`, `MEMORY/SECURITY/` |
   141→| `SetQuestionTab.hook.ts` | Set teal tab for questions | No | Kitty terminal |
   142→
   143→### SubagentStop Hooks
   144→
   145→| Hook | Purpose | Blocking | Dependencies |
   146→|------|---------|----------|--------------|
   147→| `AgentOutputCapture.hook.ts` | Capture subagent results | No | `MEMORY/STATE/` |
   148→
   149→### Stop Hooks
   150→
   151→| Hook | Purpose | Blocking | Dependencies |
   152→|------|---------|----------|--------------|
   153→| `StopOrchestrator.hook.ts` | Coordinate all Stop handlers | No | Voice Server, Kitty |
   154→
   155→### SessionEnd Hooks
   156→
   157→| Hook | Purpose | Blocking | Dependencies |
   158→|------|---------|----------|--------------|
   159→| `WorkCompletionLearning.hook.ts` | Extract learnings from work | No | Inference API, `MEMORY/LEARNING/` |
   160→| `SessionSummary.hook.ts` | Mark work as completed | No | `MEMORY/WORK/`, `current-work.json` |
   161→
   162→---
   163→
   164→## Inter-Hook Dependencies
   165→
   166→### Rating System Flow
   167→
   168→```
   169→User Message
   170→    │
   171→    ├─► ExplicitRatingCapture ─── detects "8 - great work" ───┐
   172→    │                                                         │
   173→    └─► ImplicitSentimentCapture ─── detects "amazing!" ──────┤
   174→                                                              │
   175→                                                              ▼
   176→                                              ratings.jsonl ◄───┘
   177→                                                    │
   178→                                                    ▼
   179→                                            Status Line Display
   180→                                            (statusline-command.sh)
   181→```
   182→
   183→**Coordination**: Both rating hooks write to the same `ratings.jsonl`. ExplicitRatingCapture checks for explicit patterns FIRST; if detected, ImplicitSentimentCapture defers (checks `isExplicitRating()`).
   184→
   185→### Work Tracking Flow
   186→
   187→```
   188→SessionStart
   189→    │
   190→    ▼
   191→UserPromptSubmit ─► AutoWorkCreation ─► Creates WORK/<date>/<session>/
   192→    │                                          │
   193→    │                                          ▼
   194→    │                               current-work.json (state)
   195→    │                                          │
   196→    ▼                                          │
   197→Stop ─► StopOrchestrator ─► ResponseCapture ───┤
   198→                                               │
   199→SessionEnd ─┬─► WorkCompletionLearning ────────┤
   200→            │                                  │
   201→            └─► SessionSummary ─► Marks as COMPLETED
   202→```
   203→
   204→**Coordination**: `current-work.json` is the shared state file. AutoWorkCreation creates it, ResponseCapture updates it, SessionSummary clears it.
   205→
   206→### Security Validation Flow
   207→
   208→```
   209→PreToolUse (Bash/Edit/Write/Read)
   210→    │
   211→    ▼
   212→SecurityValidator ─► patterns.yaml
   213→    │
   214→    ├─► {continue: true} ──────────────► Tool executes
   215→    │
   216→    ├─► {decision: "ask", message} ────► User prompted
   217→    │
   218→    └─► exit(2) ───────────────────────► Hard block
   219→
   220→All events logged to: MEMORY/SECURITY/security-events.jsonl
   221→```
   222→
   223→### Voice + Tab State Flow
   224→
   225→```
   226→UserPromptSubmit
   227→    │
   228→    ▼
   229→UpdateTabTitle
   230→    ├─► Sets tab to PURPLE (#5B21B6) ─► "Processing..."
   231→    │
   232→    ├─► Inference summarizes prompt
   233→    │
   234→    ├─► Sets tab to ORANGE (#B35A00) ─► "Fixing auth..."
   235→    │
   236→    └─► Voice announces: "Fixing auth bug"
   237→
   238→PreToolUse (AskUserQuestion)
   239→    │
   240→    ▼
   241→SetQuestionTab ─► Sets tab to TEAL (#085050) ─► Waiting for input
   242→
   243→Stop
   244→    │
   245→    ▼
   246→StopOrchestrator
   247→    ├─► Resets tab to DEFAULT (UL blue)
   248→    └─► Voice announces completion
   249→```
   250→
   251→---
   252→
   253→## Data Flow Diagrams
   254→
   255→### Memory System Integration
   256→
   257→```
   258→┌──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
   259→│                         MEMORY/                                  │
   260→├────────────────┬─────────────────┬───────────────────────────────┤
   261→│    WORK/       │   LEARNING/     │   STATE/                      │
   262→│                │                 │                               │
   263→│ ┌────────────┐ │ ┌─────────────┐ │ ┌───────────────────────────┐ │
   264→│ │ Session    │ │ │ SIGNALS/    │ │ │ current-work.json         │ │
   265→│ │ Directories│ │ │ ratings.jsonl│ │ │ trending-cache.json       │ │
   266→│ │            │ │ │             │ │ │ model-cache.txt           │ │
   267→│ └─────▲──────┘ │ └──────▲──────┘ │ └───────────▲───────────────┘ │
   268→│       │        │        │        │             │                 │
   269→└───────┼────────┴────────┼────────┴─────────────┼─────────────────┘
   270→        │                 │                      │
   271→        │                 │                      │
   272→┌───────┴─────────────────┴──────────────────────┴─────────────────┐
   273→│                        HOOKS                                     │
   274→│                                                                  │
   275→│  AutoWorkCreation ──────────────────────────► current-work.json  │
   276→│  ResponseCapture ───────────────────────────► WORK/ + state      │
   277→│  ExplicitRatingCapture ─────────────────────► ratings.jsonl      │
   278→│  ImplicitSentimentCapture ──────────────────► ratings.jsonl      │
   279→│  WorkCompletionLearning ────────────────────► LEARNING/          │
   280→│  SessionSummary ────────────────────────────► WORK/ + state      │
   281→│                                                                  │
   282→└──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
   283→```
   284→
   285→---
   286→
   287→## Shared Libraries
   288→
   289→Located in `hooks/lib/`:
   290→
   291→| Library | Purpose | Used By |
   292→|---------|---------|---------|
   293→| `identity.ts` | Get DA name, principal from settings | Most hooks |
   294→| `time.ts` | PST timestamps, ISO formatting | Rating hooks, work hooks |
   295→| `paths.ts` | Canonical path construction | Work hooks, security |
   296→| `notifications.ts` | Voice server + ntfy integration | Stop hooks, UpdateTabTitle |
   297→| `response-format.ts` | Tab summary validation | UpdateTabTitle |
   298→| `learning-utils.ts` | Learning categorization | Rating hooks, WorkCompletion |
   299→| `observability.ts` | Trace emitting | Future use |
   300→| `TraceEmitter.ts` | OpenTelemetry-style traces | Future use |
   301→| `IdealState.ts` | ISC tracking utilities | Algorithm integration |
   302→| `metadata-extraction.ts` | Parse assistant responses | Stop handlers |
   303→| `recovery-types.ts` | Recovery journal types | Security system |
   304→
   305→---
   306→
   307→## Configuration
   308→
   309→Hooks are configured in `settings.json` under the `hooks` key:
   310→
   311→```json
   312→{
   313→  "hooks": {
   314→    "SessionStart": [
   315→      {
   316→        "hooks": [
   317→          { "type": "command", "command": "${PAI_DIR}/hooks/StartupGreeting.hook.ts" },
   318→          { "type": "command", "command": "${PAI_DIR}/hooks/LoadContext.hook.ts" }
   319→        ]
   320→      }
   321→    ],
   322→    "PreToolUse": [
   323→      {
   324→        "matcher": "Bash",
   325→        "hooks": [
   326→          { "type": "command", "command": "${PAI_DIR}/hooks/SecurityValidator.hook.ts" }
   327→        ]
   328→      }
   329→    ]
   330→  }
   331→}
   332→```
   333→
   334→### Matcher Patterns
   335→
   336→For `PreToolUse` hooks, matchers filter by tool name:
   337→- `"Bash"` - Matches Bash tool calls
   338→- `"Edit"` - Matches Edit tool calls
   339→- `"Write"` - Matches Write tool calls
   340→- `"Read"` - Matches Read tool calls
   341→- `"AskUserQuestion"` - Matches question prompts
   342→
   343→---
   344→
   345→## Documentation Standards
   346→
   347→### Hook File Structure
   348→
   349→Every hook MUST follow this documentation structure:
   350→
   351→```typescript
   352→#!/usr/bin/env bun
   353→/**
   354→ * HookName.hook.ts - [Brief Description] ([Event Type])
   355→ *
   356→ * PURPOSE:
   357→ * [2-3 sentences explaining what this hook does and why it exists]
   358→ *
   359→ * TRIGGER: [Event type, e.g., UserPromptSubmit]
   360→ *
   361→ * INPUT:
   362→ * - [Field]: [Description]
   363→ * - [Field]: [Description]
   364→ *
   365→ * OUTPUT:
   366→ * - stdout: [What gets injected into context, if any]
   367→ * - exit(0): [Normal completion]
   368→ * - exit(2): [Hard block, for security hooks]
   369→ *
   370→ * SIDE EFFECTS:
   371→ * - [File writes]
   372→ * - [External calls]
   373→ * - [State changes]
   374→ *
   375→ * INTER-HOOK RELATIONSHIPS:
   376→ * - DEPENDS ON: [Other hooks this requires]
   377→ * - COORDINATES WITH: [Hooks that share data/state]
   378→ * - MUST RUN BEFORE: [Ordering constraints]
   379→ * - MUST RUN AFTER: [Ordering constraints]
   380→ *
   381→ * ERROR HANDLING:
   382→ * - [How errors are handled]
   383→ * - [What happens on failure]
   384→ *
   385→ * PERFORMANCE:
   386→ * - [Blocking vs async]
   387→ * - [Typical execution time]
   388→ * - [Resource usage notes]
   389→ */
   390→
   391→// Implementation follows...
   392→```
   393→
   394→### Inline Documentation
   395→
   396→Functions should have JSDoc comments explaining:
   397→- What the function does
   398→- Parameters and return values
   399→- Any side effects
   400→- Error conditions
   401→
   402→### Update Protocol
   403→
   404→When modifying ANY hook:
   405→
   406→1. Update the hook's header documentation
   407→2. Update this README's Hook Registry section
   408→3. Update Inter-Hook Dependencies if relationships change
   409→4. Update Data Flow Diagrams if data paths change
   410→5. Test the hook in isolation AND with related hooks
   411→
   412→---
   413→
   414→## Maintenance Checklist
   415→
   416→Use this checklist when adding or modifying hooks:
   417→
   418→### Adding a New Hook
   419→
   420→- [ ] Create hook file with full documentation header
   421→- [ ] Add to `settings.json` under appropriate event
   422→- [ ] Add to Hook Registry table in this README
   423→- [ ] Document inter-hook dependencies
   424→- [ ] Update Data Flow Diagrams if needed
   425→- [ ] Add to shared library imports if using lib/
   426→- [ ] Test hook in isolation
   427→- [ ] Test hook with related hooks
   428→- [ ] Verify no performance regressions
   429→
   430→### Modifying an Existing Hook
   431→
   432→- [ ] Update inline documentation
   433→- [ ] Update hook header if behavior changes
   434→- [ ] Update this README if interface changes
   435→- [ ] Update inter-hook docs if dependencies change
   436→- [ ] Test modified hook
   437→- [ ] Test hooks that depend on this hook
   438→- [ ] Verify no performance regressions
   439→
   440→### Removing a Hook
   441→
   442→- [ ] Remove from `settings.json`
   443→- [ ] Remove from Hook Registry in this README
   444→- [ ] Update inter-hook dependencies
   445→- [ ] Update Data Flow Diagrams
   446→- [ ] Check for orphaned shared state files
   447→- [ ] Delete hook file
   448→- [ ] Test related hooks still function
   449→
   450→---
   451→
   452→## Troubleshooting
   453→
   454→### Hook Not Executing
   455→
   456→1. Verify hook is in `settings.json` under correct event
   457→2. Check file is executable: `chmod +x hook.ts`
   458→3. Check shebang: `#!/usr/bin/env bun`
   459→4. Run manually: `echo '{"session_id":"test"}' | bun hooks/HookName.hook.ts`
   460→
   461→### Hook Blocking Session
   462→
   463→1. Check if hook writes to stdout (only LoadContext/FormatEnforcer should)
   464→2. Verify timeouts are set for external calls
   465→3. Check for infinite loops or blocking I/O
   466→
   467→### Security Validation Issues
   468→
   469→1. Check `patterns.yaml` for matching patterns
   470→2. Review `MEMORY/SECURITY/security-events.jsonl` for logs
   471→3. Test pattern matching: `bun hooks/SecurityValidator.hook.ts < test-input.json`
   472→
   473→---
   474→
   475→*Last updated: 2026-01-12*
   476→*Hooks count: 14 | Events: 6 | Shared libs: 11*
   477→

<system-reminder>
Whenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. You CAN and SHOULD provide analysis of malware, what it is doing. But you MUST refuse to improve or augment the code. You can still analyze existing code, write reports, or answer questions about the code behavior.
</system-reminder>

   112→### Example Bug Plan
   113→
   114→```markdown
   115→# Bug: Login Form Submits Twice on Enter
   116→
   117→## Bug Description
   118→Pressing Enter in the login form submits the form twice,
   119→causing duplicate API calls and occasional race conditions.
   120→
   121→## Problem Statement
   122→Form submission triggers twice when Enter key is pressed,
   123→resulting in duplicate POST requests to /api/auth/login.
   124→
   125→## Solution Statement
   126→Form should submit exactly once per Enter press or button click.
   127→
   128→## Steps to Reproduce
   129→1. Navigate to /login
   130→2. Enter valid credentials
   131→3. Press Enter key
   132→4. Observe Network tab: two POST requests to /api/auth/login
   133→5. Expected: one request. Actual: two requests.
   134→
   135→## Root Cause Analysis
   136→The form has both an onSubmit handler and an onClick handler
   137→on the submit button. When Enter is pressed, both fire.
   138→
   139→## Relevant Files
   140→- src/components/LoginForm.tsx
   141→- src/hooks/useAuth.ts
   142→
   143→## Step by Step Tasks
   144→1. Remove onClick handler from submit button
   145→2. Ensure onSubmit handles all submission logic
   146→3. Add form submission debounce as safety net
   147→4. Add test for single-submission behavior
   148→
   149→## Validation Commands
   150→- Run `npm test -- LoginForm` to verify component tests pass
   151→- Manually test: Enter key should trigger one request
   152→- Check Network tab confirms single POST
   153→
   154→## Notes
   155→- Check other forms for similar pattern
   156→- Consider adding global form submission tracking
   157→```markdown

   227→Add a dark mode toggle to the settings page that persists
   228→user preference and respects system settings.

   232→reduce eye strain when using the app at night.
   233→

   236→app uncomfortable to use in low-light environments.
   237→

   240→Dark, and System. Persist preference in localStorage.

content.content:
1→#!/bin/bash
     2→# Observability Dashboard Manager - Part of PAI infrastructure
     3→# Location: $PAI_DIR/Observability/ (defaults to ~/.claude/Observability/)
     4→
     5→SCRIPT_DIR="$(cd "$(dirname "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")" && pwd)"
     6→
     7→# Ensure bun is in PATH (for apps launched from macOS)
     8→export PATH="$HOME/.bun/bin:/opt/homebrew/bin:/usr/local/bin:$PATH"
     9→
    10→case "${1:-}" in
    11→    start)
    12→        # Check if already running
    13→        if lsof -Pi :4000 -sTCP:LISTEN -t >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    14→            echo "❌ Already running. Use: manage.sh restart"
    15→            exit 1
    16→        fi
    17→
    18→        # Start server (silent)
    19→        cd "$SCRIPT_DIR/apps/server"
    20→        bun run dev >/dev/null 2>&1 &
    21→        SERVER_PID=$!
    22→
    23→        # Wait for server
    24→        for i in {1..10}; do
    25→            curl -s http://localhost:4000/events/filter-options >/dev/null 2>&1 && break
    26→            sleep 1
    27→        done
    28→
    29→        # Start client (silent)
    30→        cd "$SCRIPT_DIR/apps/client"
    31→        bun run dev >/dev/null 2>&1 &
    32→        CLIENT_PID=$!
    33→
    34→        # Wait for client
    35→        for i in {1..10}; do
    36→            curl -s http://localhost:5172 >/dev/null 2>&1 && break
    37→            sleep 1
    38→        done
    39→
    40→        echo "✅ Observability running at http://localhost:5172"
    41→
    42→        # Cleanup on exit
    43→        cleanup() {
    44→            kill $SERVER_PID $CLIENT_PID 2>/dev/null
    45→            exit 0
    46→        }
    47→        trap cleanup INT
    48→        wait $SERVER_PID $CLIENT_PID
    49→        ;;
    50→
    51→    stop)
    52→        # Kill processes (silent)
    53→        for port in 4000 5172; do
    54→            if [[ "$OSTYPE" == "darwin"* ]]; then
    55→                PIDS=$(lsof -ti :$port 2>/dev/null)
    56→            else
    57→                PIDS=$(lsof -ti :$port 2>/dev/null || fuser -n tcp $port 2>/dev/null | awk '{print $2}')
    58→            fi
    59→            [ -n "$PIDS" ] && kill -9 $PIDS 2>/dev/null
    60→        done
    61→
    62→        # Kill remaining bun processes
    63→        ps aux | grep -E "bun.*(apps/(server|client))" | grep -v grep | awk '{print $2}' | while read PID; do
    64→            [ -n "$PID" ] && kill -9 $PID 2>/dev/null
    65→        done
    66→
    67→        # Clean SQLite WAL files
    68→        rm -f "$SCRIPT_DIR/apps/server/events.db-wal" "$SCRIPT_DIR/apps/server/events.db-shm" 2>/dev/null
    69→
    70→        echo "✅ Observability stopped"
    71→        ;;
    72→
    73→    restart)
    74→        echo "🔄 Restarting..."
    75→        "$0" stop 2>/dev/null
    76→        sleep 1
    77→        exec "$0" start
    78→        ;;
    79→
    80→    status)
    81→        if lsof -Pi :4000 -sTCP:LISTEN -t >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    82→            echo "✅ Running at http://localhost:5172"
    83→        else
    84→            echo "❌ Not running"
    85→        fi
    86→        ;;
    87→
    88→    start-detached)
    89→        # Check if already running
    90→        if lsof -Pi :4000 -sTCP:LISTEN -t >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    91→            echo "❌ Already running. Use: manage.sh restart"
    92→            exit 1
    93→        fi
    94→
    95→        # Start server detached (for menu bar app use)
    96→        cd "$SCRIPT_DIR/apps/server"
    97→        nohup bun run dev >/dev/null 2>&1 &
    98→        disown
    99→
   100→        # Wait for server to be ready
   101→        for i in {1..10}; do
   102→            curl -s http://localhost:4000/events/filter-options >/dev/null 2>&1 && break
   103→            sleep 1
   104→        done
   105→
   106→        # Start client detached
   107→        cd "$SCRIPT_DIR/apps/client"
   108→        nohup bun run dev >/dev/null 2>&1 &
   109→        disown
   110→
   111→        # Wait for client to be ready
   112→        for i in {1..10}; do
   113→            curl -s http://localhost:5172 >/dev/null 2>&1 && break
   114→            sleep 1
   115→        done
   116→
   117→        echo "✅ Observability running at http://localhost:5172"
   118→        ;;
   119→
   120→    *)
   121→        echo "Usage: manage.sh {start|stop|restart|status|start-detached}"
   122→        exit 1
   123→        ;;
   124→esac
   125→

1→#!/usr/bin/env bun
     2→/**
     3→ * SetQuestionTab.hook.ts - Tab Color for User Input (PreToolUse)
     4→ *
     5→ * PURPOSE:
     6→ * Changes the terminal tab color to dark teal when Claude invokes the
     7→ * AskUserQuestion tool. This visual indicator shows that the system is
     8→ * waiting for user input, distinct from the working (orange) or inference
     9→ * (purple) states set by UpdateTabTitle.
    10→ *
    11→ * TRIGGER: PreToolUse (matcher: AskUserQuestion)
    12→ *
    13→ * INPUT:
    14→ * - None (triggered by tool match, no stdin processing)
    15→ *
    16→ * OUTPUT:
    17→ * - stdout: None
    18→ * - stderr: Status message
    19→ * - exit(0): Always (non-blocking)
    20→ *
    21→ * SIDE EFFECTS:
    22→ * - Kitty remote control: Sets tab color to teal (#085050)
    23→ *
    24→ * INTER-HOOK RELATIONSHIPS:
    25→ * - DEPENDS ON: None
    26→ * - COORDINATES WITH: UpdateTabTitle (shares tab color management)
    27→ * - MUST RUN BEFORE: None
    28→ * - MUST RUN AFTER: UpdateTabTitle (overrides working color when asking)
    29→ *
    30→ * TAB COLOR SCHEME (inactive tab only - active tab stays dark blue):
    31→ * - Dark teal (#085050): Waiting for user input (this hook)
    32→ * - Dark orange (#804000): Actively working (UpdateTabTitle)
    33→ * - Dark purple (#1E0A3C): AI inference/thinking (UpdateTabTitle)
    34→ * - Dark blue (#002B80): Active tab always uses this
    35→ *
    36→ * ERROR HANDLING:
    37→ * - Kitty unavailable: Silent failure (other terminals not supported)
    38→ *
    39→ * PERFORMANCE:
    40→ * - Non-blocking: Yes
    41→ * - Typical execution: <50ms
    42→ */
    43→
    44→const TAB_AWAITING_BG = '#085050';  // Dark teal - waiting for user input
    45→const ACTIVE_TAB_BG = '#002B80';    // Dark blue - active tab always
    46→const TAB_TEXT = '#FFFFFF';
    47→const INACTIVE_TEXT = '#A0A0A0';
    48→
    49→// Simple question indicator - teal background does the work
    50→const QUESTION_TITLE = '❓ Question';
    51→
    52→async function main() {
    53→  try {
    54→    // Set tab color: active stays dark blue, inactive shows teal
    55→    await Bun.$`kitten @ set-tab-color --self active_bg=${ACTIVE_TAB_BG} active_fg=${TAB_TEXT} inactive_bg=${TAB_AWAITING_BG} inactive_fg=${INACTIVE_TEXT}`;
    56→
    57→    // Set simple question title - teal background provides visual distinction
    58→    await Bun.$`kitty @ set-tab-title ${QUESTION_TITLE}`;
    59→
    60→    console.error('[SetQuestionTab] Tab set to teal with question indicator');
    61→  } catch (error) {
    62→    // Silently fail if kitty remote control is not available
    63→    console.error('[SetQuestionTab] Kitty remote control unavailable');
    64→  }
    65→
    66→  process.exit(0);
    67→}
    68→
    69→main();
    70→
